content: We can reframe curiosity-driven autonomous agents to treat failed trajectories of environmental exploration as a self-supervised 'intrinsic failure signal' that augments existing curiosity mechanisms like ICM and RND, rather than only rewarding discovery of new states. This creates an intrinsic reward incentive for agents to revisit and debug uncompleted attempts, instead of abandoning high-potential incomplete trajectories to chase only novelty.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
